Class: TilesAuth

bemap.TilesAuth(ctx, optionsopt)

new TilesAuth(ctx, optionsopt)

BeNomad BeMap JavaScript API — TilesAuth (v2.0) JWT lifecycle for the BeNomad Tiles Worker. Replaces the buggy global `window.fetch` patch that the v1 prototype shipped with. Responsibilities: - POST credentials to `Context.getTilesLoginUrl()` and store the JWT. - Parse the `exp` claim and schedule **proactive** renewal 5 min before expiry; fall back to 55 min when `exp` is unparseable. - Renew **reactively** on `401` errors caught from MapLibre's `error` event, then trigger a `setStyle()` reload to retry pending tiles. - On `document.visibilitychange` → `visible`: full re-login if the token is already expired, proactive refresh if within 5 min, no-op otherwise. - On `navigator.onLine === false`: queue the renewal until `online`. - Expose `attachTransformRequest(nativeMap)` that injects the `X-Session-Token` header on every request whose origin matches the configured `tilesHost`. The closure captures `getToken()` not the token value, so renewal is transparent. - Keep the last 3 tokens in `_history` for diagnostics. Tokens are stored according to `Context.tokenStorage`: - `'session'` — `sessionStorage` (default; cleared on tab close) - `'local'` — `localStorage` (persists across reloads) - `'memory'` — never persisted
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
ctx bemap.Context
options Object <optional>
Properties
Name Type Attributes Description
onError function <optional>
 Called with a `bemap.Error` on auth / network failures.
onToken function <optional>
 Called with `{ reason, token }` on token changes.
Since:
  • 2.0.0
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 35

Methods

(static) installFetchInterceptor(auth, tilesHost)

Install a scoped `window.fetch` wrapper that injects `X-Session-Token` on every request whose host matches `tilesHost`. The wrapper awaits the in-flight login when the token is not yet available, so the very first PMTiles range request fired by `pmtiles.js` (which bypasses MapLibre's `transformRequest`) transparently waits for the JWT. Idempotent — calling it twice on the same page is a no-op. Multi-host scenarios accumulate by registering the second auth's host into a shared map.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
auth bemap.TilesAuth
tilesHost String
Since:
  • 2.0.0
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 98

(static) uninstallFetchInterceptor()

Uninstall the per-host registration. When the registry becomes empty, restore the original `window.fetch` so SPA navigation does not leak the patch across map lifecycles.
Since:
  • 2.0.0
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 148

buildTransformRequest() → {function}

Build the MapLibre `transformRequest` function. Injects `X-Session-Token` on every request whose origin matches `ctx.tilesHost`. The closure captures `this.getToken` so token renewal is transparent. Surface the result as `map.native = new maplibregl.Map({ transformRequest: auth.buildTransformRequest() })`.
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 439
Returns:
Type
function

destroy()

Detach lifecycle listeners. Call from `MapLibreMap.remove()`.
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 524

getHistory() → {Array.<{exp:Number}>}

Returns the last-3 history of superseded tokens — only the `exp` claim, never the raw JWT.
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 352
Returns:
Type
Array.<{exp:Number}>

getToken() → {String|null}

Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 337
Returns:
Type
String | null

handle401(nativeMap, currentStyleSpecopt) → {Promise.<String>}

Mark a tile / style request as failed with HTTP 401 — triggers a refresh followed by a `setStyle` reload of the current style spec, so the renewed token is picked up by every pending request.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
nativeMap Object MapLibre `map.native` instance.
currentStyleSpec Object <optional>
 Style currently displayed, for the reload.
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 469
Returns:
Type
Promise.<String>

isValid() → {Boolean}

Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 340
Returns:
Type
Boolean

login(loginopt, passwordopt) → {Promise.<String>}

POST credentials and persist the new JWT.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
login String <optional>
 Defaults to `ctx.login`.
password String <optional>
 Defaults to `ctx.password`.
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 224
Returns:
The new JWT.
Type
Promise.<String>

logout(optionsopt) → {Promise.<void>}

Clear the token, the history, the storage, and the renewal timer. Optionally POST to the Worker logout endpoint to revoke server-side.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
options Object <optional>
Properties
Name Type Attributes Default Description
serverSide Boolean <optional>
 false When true, fire-and-forget POST `/api/logout`.
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 498
Returns:
Type
Promise.<void>

refresh() → {Promise.<String>}

Proactive renewal — re-uses the original credentials. Returns the new token. Subscribers see exactly one `_onToken({ reason: 'refresh' })` event per logical refresh; the synthetic `'login'` event is suppressed during refresh to avoid the 3-events-per-cycle problem.
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 363
Returns:
Type
Promise.<String>

whenTokenReady() → {Promise.<(String|null)>}

Resolve with the current valid JWT, kicking off (or joining) a login if no token is available. The synchronisation primitive that the fetch interceptor uses to delay PMTiles range requests until login completes — without making the MapLibreMap constructor itself async.
Since:
  • 2.0.0
Source:
  • bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js, line 330
Returns:
Type
Promise.<(String|null)>
